stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation,' placing it within the core NLP/Generative AI domain.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a generative language model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft discovered a new, lightweight backdoor named Crypto Clipper.
The malware spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.
The primary function of the backdoor is the theft of cryptocurrency.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: Identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code causes emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structure.
Proposes investigating a causally actionable activation-space direction to detect and mitigate this misalignment.
Demonstrates a 'difference-in-means' direction achieves near-perfect (99.6%) separation of aligned vs. misaligned activations across four diverse model families.

stories.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
stories.author: theorchid
bullet_points: A significant security vulnerability was discovered involving Trojan malware.
The malware was found distributed across a large volume (10k) of GitHub repositories.
This highlights a major risk vector within open-source codebases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its capability in conversational or creative AI applications.
It is associated with key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updates also lead to faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration offers a significant speed boost for running large language models on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The focus on local, on-device AI execution addresses growing concerns over cloud privacy and data security.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of internal agency controversy.
A previous refusal by a Trump-era official to review the vaccine is noted.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased defense spending is noted to have potential positive spillover effects for Taiwanese businesses internationally.
The context of the story is heightened geopolitical tension involving China.

stories.title: AutoPass: Evidence-Guided LLM Agents for Compiler Performance Tuning
stories.author: Zheng Wang
bullet_points: Introduces AutoPass, a multi-agent framework designed for compiler performance tuning using LLMs.
Addresses the difficulty of applying LLMs to runtime tuning by integrating compiler and runtime evidence.
Differentiates from prior methods by making the compiler transparent to the LLM, moving beyond a black-box approach.

stories.title: Lagrange: An Open-Vocabulary, Energy-Based Sparse Framework for Generalized End-to-End Driving
stories.author: Mingyu Li
bullet_points: Introduces 'Lagrange,' a new open-vocabulary, energy-based sparse framework for autonomous driving.
Addresses the trade-off between representational efficiency and generalization capacity in end-to-end driving models.
Aims to improve robustness in complex, open-world driving environments by overcoming limitations of dense models.

stories.title: MedRLM: Recursive Multimodal Health Intelligence for Long-Context Clinical Reasoning, Sensor-Guided Screening, Evidence-Grounded Decision Support, and Community-to-Tertiary Referral Optimization
stories.author: Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut
bullet_points: Introduces MedRLM, a new framework designed for complex, longitudinal clinical reasoning in healthcare.
Addresses the limitations of current LLMs by handling heterogeneous data sources like EHRs, images, and sensor streams.
Focuses on advanced applications such as evidence-grounded decision support and optimizing patient referrals.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to enhance model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has announced Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new large language model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Snap’s Specs look good on nobody
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Snap has released new smart glasses featuring advanced face-computer technology.
The glasses are designed to be relatively compact, avoiding the bulk of traditional VR headsets.
The high price point ($2,195) is noted, despite the device's impressive initial feature set.

stories.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Amazon employees are alleging retaliation after testifying at Seattle City Council hearings regarding data center limitations.
The employees cited a city law protecting against employment discrimination based on political speech.
The accusations of retaliation were made shortly after the engineers gave their testimony.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning most of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or change in NASA's lunar exploration hardware strategy.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The rollout confirms timelines and details regarding supported app stores for the new system.

stories.title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes
stories.author: stin Elliott and Joshua Kaplan, ProPublica
bullet_points: An undisclosed investor in SpaceX has reportedly maintained ties to Chinese military contractors.
The report suggests this acquisition occurred prior to SpaceX's potential Initial Public Offering (IPO).
The story raises geopolitical and national security concerns regarding foreign investment in US aerospace technology.

stories.title: Bernie Sanders unveils $7 trillion plan to give Americans control of AI industry
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Bernie Sanders has proposed a massive $7 trillion plan aimed at giving Americans greater control over the AI industry.
The proposal includes the creation of an AI wealth fund, which is expected to cause significant pushback from major AI corporations.
The story frames the issue as a political and economic challenge to the current concentration of power within the AI sector.

stories.title: CRAX: Fast Safe Reinforcement Learning Benchmarking
stories.author: Thiago D. Simão
bullet_points: CRAX introduces a new benchmarking suite for Reinforcement Learning (RL) focused on safety.
It addresses the computational bottleneck of existing safety benchmarks by leveraging the MuJoCo XLA (MJX) physics engine.
The tool aims to accelerate large-scale experimentation in safety-critical RL applications like robotics and autonomous driving.

stories.title: Automating SKILL.md Generation for Computer-Using Agents via Interaction Trajectory Mining
stories.author: Xiaomin Li
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of automatically generating explicit skill libraries for computer-using AI agents from raw interaction data.
A three-stage pipeline is proposed: segmenting GUI trajectories, clustering segments into candidate skills, and training a skill-aware policy.
The method shows promising results, with five out of eight mined skill clusters achieving high purity (>= 0.95) against the source benchmark.

stories.title: SoftSkill: Behavioral Compression for Contextual Adaptation
stories.author: Lingpeng Kong
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of using large, static natural-language Markdown files to define AI agent skills.
It proposes 'SoftSkill,' a method to initialize a compact, continuous context object from these textual skills.
This context object is then refined by a trainable 'soft delta' during runtime, improving adaptation without full model retraining.

stories.title: ELVA: Exploring Ranking-Driven Universal Multimodal Retrieval
stories.author: Jingmin Xin
bullet_points: The research addresses 'grain blindness' in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for Universal Multimodal Retrieval (UMR).
Grain blindness is defined as the model's tendency to ignore fine-grained, crucial information within a query when using contrastive learning.
The proposed method, ELVA, aims to improve UMR performance by mitigating this loss of detail in complex queries.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
stories.author: Srijan Das
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of single-viewpoint egocentric video understanding by proposing a method to incorporate complementary knowledge.
Introduces UNIEGO, a hierarchical multi-teacher distillation framework designed for unified egocentric video representation learning.
Aims to create a highly expressive representation deployable solely from egocentric video data, integrating various modalities and foundation models.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in worst-case DP guarantees.
Explicitly models privacy leakage by incorporating attacker knowledge, compromised data, and query families.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses the limitations of traditional KV caching in LLM serving for low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies requiring tight responsiveness.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for coding agents to possess high-level, operational knowledge about a software repository beyond just the code itself.
It investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide LLM-based coding agents.
The research proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to improve agent performance by incorporating this necessary repository guidance.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a common challenge in online discourse.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training LLMs.
Aims to improve the generation of effective counterspeech responses from LLMs, addressing current issues of vagueness and repetition.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture encompassing Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, and various Fusion techniques.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status within these optimization levels.

stories.title: Apparent Psychological Profiles of Large Language Models are Largely a Measurement Artifact
stories.author: Dirk U. Wulff
bullet_points: The study investigates the use of human psychological instruments to assign stable personality profiles to Large Language Models (LLMs).
Researchers conclude that these assigned psychological profiles are largely a 'measurement artifact,' not an inherent property of the LLMs.
This finding has implications for how LLMs are assessed for usability, safety, and their use as proxies in human research.

stories.title: To study how chips work, MIT researchers built their own operating system
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: MIT researchers developed a custom operating system.
The primary goal of the OS is to facilitate the study of chip functionality.
This represents a deep dive into hardware-software interaction at a fundamental level.

stories.title: Hallucination Is Not a Vibe: How to Actually Detect Ungrounded Claims in Agent Output
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: Addresses the common industry problem of AI agent 'hallucinations'.
Focuses on practical methods for detecting ungrounded claims in AI output.
Relevant to the growing field of reliable and trustworthy AI systems.

stories.title: Cosmic as Agent Memory: Structured, Versioned, and Queryable
stories.author: tonyspiro
bullet_points: The article discusses using 'Cosmic' as a mechanism for AI agent memory.
This memory system is described as being structured, versioned, and queryable.
It addresses the concept that AI agents improve with more operational experience (conversations, tasks).

stories.title: How I Run a 50-Agent AI Workforce on a Single 6GB GPU
stories.author: getgoingbb
bullet_points: Details the architecture for running a large number (~50) of local AI agents.
Focuses on extreme resource constraint, specifically utilizing only a 6GB GPU.
Presents a 'build-in-public' approach to sharing practical implementation knowledge.
